festivals.name: Inti Raymi / San Juan
festivals.description: Indigenous Kichwa communities celebrate the summer solstice with dancing, ceremonial bathing, and offerings around Otavalo.

festivals.name: Carnaval de Guaranda
festivals.description: Ecuador's most traditional carnival fills Guaranda with music, costumes, and the inevitable water and flour battles.

destinations.name: Galápagos Islands
destinations.description: The living laboratory of evolution where giant tortoises, marine iguanas, and blue-footed boobies evolved without fear of man.

destinations.name: Quito
destinations.description: The world's highest UNESCO colonial capital sits at 2,850m with the best-preserved Spanish colonial centre in Latin America.

destinations.name: Amazon (Tena/Napo)
destinations.description: Ecuador's accessible Amazon basin offers wildlife spotting, indigenous community visits, and white-water kayaking.

destinations.name: Otavalo & Imbabura
destinations.description: Saturday's Otavalo market is South America's largest indigenous market, surrounded by volcanic lakes and craft villages.

destinations.name: Avenue of Volcanoes
destinations.description: The Andes corridor between Quito and Cuenca is flanked by 10 volcanoes including Cotopaxi and Chimborazo.

destinations.name: Cuenca
destinations.description: Ecuador's most charming colonial city with blue-domed cathedral and nearby Ingapirca Inca ruins.

destinations.name: Mindo Cloudforest
destinations.description: Ecuador's premier birding destination with over 500 species in a misty cloud forest just 2 hours from Quito.

destinations.name: Cuyabeno Wildlife Reserve
destinations.description: One of the most biodiverse reserves in the Amazon with river travel through black-water lagoons teeming with pink dolphins.
